title: 2026 predictions with Robert Armstrong
channel: The Prof G Pod – Scott Galloway

transcript.text: So Rob, you are one of our first guests
of the year. So we want to get your take
on what is in store for 2026. Are we in
a bubble and will the bubble burst?
>> Well, that's just numbers, right? Either
you believe in valuations or you don't.
I believe in them. We're in the 95th
percentile, the 97th percentile of how
expensive risk assets are related to
history. And always before in history,
that predicts low returns over the next
10 years or so. But the problem is that
long term. Do we have that big step down
in 2026 or in 2031 or one year in
between? I have no idea. And while I'm
waiting around for it to happen, I'm
giving up returns. So if it happens 3
years from now and I've been sitting on
the sideline not owning stocks, what
have I achieved? The probability is we
won't have a pop this year because most
years we don't, right? [laughter]
You know, it's it's that dumb, right?
[laughter]
Is that really your reasoning? Because
most years we don't.
>> Most years we don't. You know, even at
these levels of high valuation, these
things can go on along for quite a long
time. The economy is strong. We can talk
about the somewhat wobbly labor market,
which is a red flag. But in terms of
growth and spending and activity, by a
lot of measures, things are good and
perhaps even getting better. We might
have Fed cuts coming. We definitely have
massive fiscal stimulus coming. It's an
environment that is quite benign, which
means there's less possible triggers for
the deflation of the asset bubble that
we are in right now.
